# Conversations in Philosophy: 'The Thing' by Martin Heidegger

[Close Readings](https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/close-readings/id1669485143)

Play

What does it mean for a jug to be a jug? Or for any thing to be called a вАШthingвАЩ? In his 1950 lecture вАШDas DingвАЩ, Heidegger attempts to cajole his audience away from their everyday way of seeing the world as consisting of objects that can be represented objectively, and into the kind of thinking that вАШresponds and recallsвАЩ. For Heidegger, the world we experience is one of dynamic movement between revelation and concealment, where the essential nature of a thing lies in its вАШthingingвАЩ, and the вАШjugвАЩs jug character consists in the poured gift of the jugвАЩs pouring outвАЩ. In this episode Jonathan and James work through HeideggerвАЩs ideas about both вАШthingsвАЩ and time, and consider the purpose of his poetic style.
